title: Therapeutic microsphere formulations containing end-capped polymers
abstract: Provided herein are methods of preparing microspheres (e.g., single emulsion microspheres) comprising a therapeutic compound or pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof and a poly(lactic-coglycolic acid) (PLGA) polymer comprising at least one hydrophobic terminus, as well as microspheres and pharmaceutical compositions related thereto.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of preparing a plurality of single emulsion microspheres, comprising: a) combining a first solvent and a therapeutic compound or pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof to form a first mixture; b) combining a second solvent and a polymer to form a second mixture, wherein the polymer comprises poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA) comprising at least one hydrophobic terminus, and wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of at least 7 kD; c) combining the first and second mixtures to form a feed; d) dispersing the combined first and second mixtures of step (c) into an aqueous continuous phase to form a plurality of droplets; and e) hardening the plurality of droplets formed in step (d) to form the plurality of single emulsion microspheres, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 20 pm and 42 pm in diameter. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of less than 54 kD. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the polymer has a ratio of lactide:glycolide of 50:50. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises a glucocorticoid. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises triamcinolone. 8 The method of claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the first solvent comprises DMA. 9. The method of any one of claims 6-8, wherein the feed produced in step (c) comprises the therapeutic compound or pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof at a concentration of at least 20mg/mL. 10. The method of any one of claims 6-9, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 22 pm and 34 pm in diameter. 12. The method of claim 10 or claim 11, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 4% by total weight of the microspheres. 13. The method of any one of claims 6-9, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 20 pm and 32 pm in diameter. 15. The method of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 6% by total weight of the microspheres. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises an anesthetic. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises bupivacaine. 18. The method of claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the first solvent comprises benzyl alcohol. 19. The method of any one of claims 16-18, wherein the feed produced in step (c) comprises the therapeutic compound or pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof at a concentration of at least 40mg/mL. 20. The method of any one of claims 16-19, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 22 pm and 36 pm in diameter. 22. The method of claim 20 or claim 21, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 5% by total weight of the microspheres. 23. The method of any one of claims 16-19, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 24 pm and 42 pm in diameter. 25. The method of claim 23 or claim 24, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 9% by total weight of the microspheres. 26. The method of any one of claims 1-25, further comprising adjusting the pH of the feed prior to step (d). 27. The method of any one of claims 1-26, further comprising adjusting the pH of the aqueous continuous phase into which the feed is dispersed in step (d). 28. The method of any one of claims 1-27, further comprising, after step (d) and prior to step (e), washing the plurality of microspheres in a second aqueous continuous phase. 29. A plurality of microspheres produced by the method of any one of claims 1-28. 30. A plurality of single emulsion microspheres, wherein each microsphere of the plurality comprises: a therapeutic compound or pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; and a polymer, wherein the polymer comprises poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA) comprising at least one hydrophobic terminus, and wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of at least 38 kD; wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 20 pm and 42 pm in diameter. 31. The plurality of microspheres of claim 30, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight of less than 54 kD. 32. The plurality of microspheres of claim 31, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 33. The plurality of microspheres of claim 31, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 34. The plurality of microspheres of any one of claims 30-33, wherein the polymer has a ratio of lactide:glycolide of 50:50. 35. The plurality of microspheres of any one of claims 30-34, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises a glucocorticoid. 36. The plurality of microspheres of claim 35, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises triamcinolone. 37. The plurality of microspheres of claim 35 or claim 36, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 38. The plurality of microspheres of claim 37, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 22 pm and 34 pm in diameter. 39. The plurality of microspheres of claim 37 or claim 38, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 4% by total weight of the microspheres. 40. The plurality of microspheres of claim 35 or claim 36, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 41. The plurality of microspheres of claim 40, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 20 pm and 32 pm in diameter. 42. The plurality of microspheres of claim 40 or claim 41, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 6% by total weight of the microspheres. 43. The plurality of microspheres of any one of claims 30-34, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises an anesthetic. 44. The plurality of microspheres of claim 43, wherein the therapeutic compound comprises bupivacaine. 45. The plurality of microspheres of claim 43 or claim 44, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 7 kD and 17 kD. 46. The plurality of microspheres of claim 45, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 22 pm and 36 pm in diameter. 47. The plurality of microspheres of claim 45 or claim 46, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 5% by total weight of the microspheres. 48. The plurality of microspheres of claim 43 or claim 44, wherein the polymer has a molecular weight between 38 kD and 54 kD. 49. The plurality of microspheres of claim 48, wherein at least 90% of the microspheres of the plurality are between 24 pm and 42 pm in diameter. 50. The plurality of microspheres of claim 48 or claim 49, wherein the therapeutic compound or salt is greater than 9% by total weight of the microspheres. 51. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the plurality of microspheres of any one of claims 29-50.
